claim: LangSmith traces enable code agents to fetch and reflect on agent execution history to improve prompts, tools, and context engineering
actionable: Connect LangSmith as observability layer to Claude Code or Deep Agents; use langsmith-fetch CLI to retrieve traces for prompt iteration
source_title: The agent development loop with LangSmith + Claude Code / Deepagents

claim: LangSmith Fetch CLI allows code agents to retrieve recent traces without manual intervention; agents learn to use help commands to discover CLI capabilities
actionable: Use langsmith-fetch to programmatically fetch traces; agents can introspect traces to guide prompt improvements or tool design
source_title: The agent development loop with LangSmith + Claude Code / Deepagents

claim: Claude Code skills encapsulate tool knowledge; creating a skill for LangSmith Fetch teaches Claude how to use the CLI without repeating the setup each session
actionable: Create Claude skills that encapsulate CLI tools like langsmith-fetch; share skills across sessions
source_title: The agent development loop with LangSmith + Claude Code / Deepagents

claim: Deep Agents CLI supports dual tracing: agent-specific traces separate from code execution traces; this separation helps debug agent logic independently from code logic
actionable: Use environment variables to configure separate LangSmith projects for agent reasoning and code execution traces
source_title: The agent development loop with LangSmith + Claude Code / Deepagents

claim: Shared CLI access across code agents and traditional coding workflows allows both to benefit from the same observability infrastructure
actionable: Install langsmith-fetch globally; make it available as a skill to both Claude Code and Deep Agents for unified trace access
source_title: The agent development loop with LangSmith + Claude Code / Deepagents
